chapter_title.en: The Prostration of Gratitude (sujood Al-shukr) and What to Say in it

text: 968 - وَ رَوَى جَهْمُ بْنُ أَبِي جَهْمٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ قَدْ سَجَدَ بَعْدَ اَلثَّلاَثِ اَلرَّكَعَاتِ مِنَ اَلْمَغْرِبِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ رَأَيْتُكَ سَجَدْتَ بَعْدَ اَلثَّلاَثِ فَقَالَ «وَ رَأَيْتَنِي» فَقُلْتُ نَعَمْ قَالَ «فَلاَ تَدَعْهَا فَإِنَّ اَلدُّعَاءَ فِيهَا مُسْتَجَابٌ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.968 - Jahm ibn Abi Jahm narrated that he saw Imam Musa ibn Ja'far (as) performing a prostration after the three rak'ahs of Maghrib prayer.
He said: "May I be your ransom! I saw you prostrate after the three [rak'ahs]."
Imam (as) replied: "And you observed me doing that?"
Jahm said: "Yes."
The Imam (as) then said: "Do not abandon it, for the supplication made during it is answered."
